prerequisites: Access to User Settings in your Codefresh account so you can generate an API key.
A Codefresh account identifier (account_id) and a start_date to provide to the connector.

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: Codefresh authenticates with an API key passed in the Authorization header. The connector collects this as the api_key secret field, plus an account_id that identifies the Codefresh account to read from. Generate the key in User Settings and select the access scopes the connector needs.

setupSteps.title: Generate a Codefresh API key
setupSteps.body: Log in to Codefresh and select User Settings from your avatar dropdown. Scroll down to API Keys and click Generate. Select the required access scopes, then copy the generated token to your clipboard. Treat the token as sensitive.

setupSteps.title: Provide the account ID and API key
setupSteps.body: Enter your Codefresh account_id and the generated api_key into the connector. Both are required secret credentials. The api_key is sent in the Authorization header on each request to the Codefresh API (https://g.codefresh.io/api/).

setupSteps.title: Set the sync start date
setupSteps.body: Set start_date (required) to the earliest date from which the connector should begin pulling data.

setupSteps.title: Configure optional report settings
setupSteps.body: Optionally set report_date_range and report_granularity to control the date window and aggregation level of the data the connector retrieves. Leave them unset to use the connector defaults.
